_meta.purpose: Forward-looking capital market assumptions for the ETF-investable asset classes a Singapore retail investor can access. Synthesised house estimates, informed by published long-run (10-15yr) assumptions from major asset managers (2025-26 vintage). NOT a reproduction of any single provider's proprietary table. NOT a forecast. Educational use only.
_meta.return_basis: Geometric, per annum. USD-basis except classes marked basis=SGD. SGD-based investors face an additional FX consideration on USD-basis classes (~ +/-1% p.a. unhedged, or the hedging cost) — surfaced in-app, not baked into these numbers.
_meta.risk_free_note: USD cash anchor used for Sharpe across all classes for consistency. SGD cash ~2.2% long-run (structurally below USD; MAS runs exchange-rate policy). Sharpe = (ret - risk_free) / vol.
_meta.confidence: Round-number estimates. Treat as directional, not precise. Wide bands apply. These are LONG-RUN AVERAGES over the horizon, not today's rates — short-rate classes (cash, SGD bonds) in particular will differ from current board/promotional rates in either direction.
_meta.revision_log: 2026-07-09: cash 3.0->2.2 (2024-25 ~3% SGD money-market was a cyclical high, not a structural level; SGD short rates run below USD long-run) and sgd_bonds 3.2->3.0 (restores a sensible ~80bp term/credit premium over cash; consistent with SGS + SGD IG starting yields). All other classes reviewed and kept.

dev_equity.label: Global / Developed Equity
dev_equity.role: Core growth engine (world & US developed markets).
dev_equity.informed_by: US large cap 5.5-7.0% + non-US developed 7.5-8.5%, cap-weighted blend.

sg_equity.label: Singapore Equity
sg_equity.informed_by: STI dividend yield ~4.5-5.0% plus modest long-run earnings growth; SGD basis, so most of the return arrives as income.

em_asia_equity.label: EM & Asia ex-Japan Equity
em_asia_equity.role: Higher-growth diversifier; higher volatility, cheaper valuations.

thematic_equity.label: Thematic / Sector Equity
thematic_equity.role: Concentrated sector/theme bets (tech, EV, low-carbon). No return premium assumed over broad equity; much higher idiosyncratic risk. Satellite only.
thematic_equity.informed_by: Broad equity return with a large volatility uplift for concentration.

dev_bonds.label: Global / US Aggregate Bonds
dev_bonds.role: Core ballast; flight-to-quality in equity sell-offs.

sgd_bonds.label: SGD Bonds (SG Govt / IG)
sgd_bonds.role: SGD-denominated ballast; no currency risk for a SG investor.
sgd_bonds.informed_by: Starting yields: SGS ~2.6-2.8% blended with SGD investment-grade ~3.3-3.5%; ~80bp term/credit premium over long-run SGD cash.

asia_bonds.label: Asia / EM Bonds
asia_bonds.role: Higher-yield credit; note higher equity correlation than govt bonds.

reits.label: REITs (SG / Asia)
reits.role: Listed real-estate income; inflation-sensitive, equity-like in stress.
reits.informed_by: S-REIT distribution yield ~5.5-6.0% plus modest DPU growth net of dilution; listed-market volatility.

gold.label: Gold
gold.role: Tail-risk hedge; near-zero equity correlation; central-bank demand.

cash.label: Cash (SGD)
cash.role: Liquidity buffer; opportunity cost in bull markets.
cash.informed_by: Long-run SGD money-market (SORA-linked) average. SGD short rates run structurally below USD because MAS manages the exchange rate, not interest rates; the ~3% seen in 2024-25 was a cyclical high, not a level to plan on.

_corr_note: Pairwise correlations, informed by ~20yr exponentially-weighted history from the source SAA work. Symmetric; diagonal = 1; any pair not listed defaults to 0.30 and is reported by the pipeline so nothing is silently assumed. In crisis regimes equity-credit and equity-REIT correlations spike toward 0.85+.

us_estate._note: US estate (inheritance) tax on US-situs assets held by a Singapore-resident non-resident-alien. Situs follows the underlying asset, NOT the broker. Educational; not tax advice.
us_estate.source: Financial Horse, 'Die holding US Stocks and pay 40% tax?' (3 Jul 2026); cross-checked to IRC 2001(c) graduated schedule. Singapore has no US estate-tax treaty.

us_div_withholding._note: Withholding tax suffered on US-source dividends INSIDE the fund, by fund domicile. Irish/Lux UCITS get the 15% treaty rate; US-domiciled funds held by a Singaporean suffer 30%; synthetic (swap-based) funds can reduce this further but are flagged separately. Applied only to the US-dividend portion of a fund's income.
us_div_withholding._content_note: us_content = fraction of the fund's income that is US-source and subject to US DIVIDEND withholding. Estimates. drag = assumed_div_yield * us_content * wht_rate. Bond funds are set near-zero: US-source INTEREST paid to a foreign fund is largely exempt under the US portfolio-interest exemption, so a global aggregate suffers little US withholding despite ~40% US content. Models the dominant US-dividend case only; non-US withholding (e.g. China ~10%) is not modelled and is flagged where material.
